steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Both terms in $46x+46y$ contain the factor $46$. Since the same number multiplies $x$ and $y$, that number can be pulled outside one set of parentheses.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
Pull $46$ outside the parentheses and keep the two unlike terms inside. The expression inside the parentheses must still show both variables.
$$46x+46y=46(x+y)$$

steps.title: Match the equivalent expression
steps.content: The factored form preserves the original addition of two separate terms instead of multiplying the variables or dropping one variable. The answer is B.
